req.title: Create patient (negative)
req.statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-051 targets the POST /Patient endpoint with clinician-role auth. We need: (1) a positive EP case proving a fully-valid payload yields 201; (2) a negative NEG/DT case proving a payload missing the mandatory 'name' element yields 422; (3) a boundary BVA case probing the minimum-length name string (single character family name) to confirm acceptance at the exact lower bound; (4) a STC case verifying that attempting to re-POST a patient whose identifier already exists triggers a conflict/invalid state response; (5) a DT case exercising the permission-denial path where a non-clinician role is rejected. Together these cover all error pathways cited in the requirement.

tests.name: test_create_patient_valid_demographics_returns_201
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Caller holds role:clinician with valid auth token
No pre-existing patient with the test identifier exists
actions: Build a FHIR Patient payload with all mandatory elements: resourceType, identifier, name (family + given), gender, birthDate
POST payload to /Patient with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an 'id' assigned by the server
trace: R-HLT-051: patient resource stored on valid creation
EP: valid-input partition — all mandatory elements present
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_valid_demographics_returns_201(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    base_url,                # <fixture>
    unique_identifier,       # <fixture>
    cleanup_patient,         # <fixture>
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "name": [{"family": "Smith", "given": ["Alice"]}],
        "gender": "female",
        "birthDate": "1990-06-15"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert "id" in body
    cleanup_patient(body["id"])

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_name_returns_422
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Caller holds role:clinician with valid auth token
actions: Build a FHIR Patient payload omitting the mandatory 'name' element
POST payload to /Patient with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error indicating 'name' is required
trace: R-HLT-051: creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected
R-HLT-051: error pathway — 422 when mandatory element missing
NEG: invalid-input partition — mandatory field absent
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_missing_name_returns_422(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    base_url,                # <fixture>
    unique_identifier,       # <fixture>
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "1985-03-22"
        # 'name' intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "name" in error_text or "mandatory" in error_text or "required" in error_text

tests.name: test_create_patient_single_char_family_name_accepted
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Caller holds role:clinician with valid auth token
Server minimum family-name length is 1 character per FHIR spec
actions: Build a valid FHIR Patient payload where 'family' name is exactly 1 character ('A')
POST payload to /Patient with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 201 Created — single-character family name is at the lower boundary and must be accepted
trace: R-HLT-051: validated demographics — name field lower-bound length
BVA: minimum boundary value of family name string length (len=1)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_single_char_family_name_accepted(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    base_url,                # <fixture>
    unique_identifier,       # <fixture>
    cleanup_patient,         # <fixture>
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "name": [{"family": "A", "given": ["B"]}],  # boundary: 1-char family
        "gender": "unknown",
        "birthDate": "2000-01-01"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    cleanup_patient(body["id"])

tests.name: test_create_patient_non_clinician_role_returns_403
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Caller holds role:receptionist (not clinician) with valid auth token
actions: Build a fully-valid FHIR Patient payload with all mandatory elements
POST payload to /Patient using receptionist auth header (non-clinician role)
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden — permission denied for non-clinician role
No patient resource created on the server
trace: R-HLT-051: negative path — permission denial must be tested
DT: decision rule — role NOT IN {clinician} → deny creation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_non_clinician_role_returns_403(
    receptionist_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    base_url,                   # <fixture>
    unique_identifier,          # <fixture>
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "name": [{"family": "Jones", "given": ["Bob"]}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "1975-11-30"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=receptionist_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403

tests.name: test_create_patient_duplicate_identifier_rejected
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Caller holds role:clinician with valid auth token
A patient with the target identifier already exists (state: patient-exists)
actions: POST a valid Patient payload to /Patient to establish the existing record (transition to state: patient-exists)
Immediately POST the identical payload a second time using the same identifier
Capture the second response status and body
expected: Second POST returns HTTP 409 Conflict or HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity — conflicting state transition rejected
trace: R-HLT-051: conflicting state transitions must be tested
STC: state transition from patient-exists back to patient-create must be blocked
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_duplicate_identifier_rejected(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    base_url,                # <fixture>
    unique_identifier,       # <fixture>
    cleanup_patient,         # <fixture>
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": unique_identifier}],
        "name": [{"family": "Dupont", "given": ["Claire"]}],
        "gender": "female",
        "birthDate": "1982-07-04"
    }
    first = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert first.status_code == 201
    cleanup_patient(first.json()["id"])

    second = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert second.status_code in (409, 422)
